about.title: About & Acknowledgments
about.subtitle: Justivia Graduation Project - Algerian Administrative Legal Intelligence
about.description: This platform was developed as an advanced legal artificial intelligence tool, specialized in analyzing and interpreting Algerian administrative laws and regulations to facilitate access to legal information for researchers and legal practitioners.
about.acknowledgmentText: With feelings of gratitude and appreciation, we extend our sincere thanks and praise to Dr. Misbahia Nadia for her tireless supervision and wise guidance, which served as a beacon throughout our research journey. We also thank our honorable families and everyone who supported and encouraged us to complete this humble work in the best possible way.

terms.title: Terms of Service & Services
terms.subtitle: Rules governing the use of the Justivia Legal Intelligence platform
terms.intro: Your use of the Justivia platform is subject to these terms and conditions. By browsing the platform or using the AI Legal Consultant, you agree to comply fully with these terms under the legislative framework of the People's Democratic Republic of Algeria.
terms.section1Title: 1. Nature of the Service & Academic Frame
terms.section1Text: Justivia is an interactive AI simulation platform developed as a graduation project at the University of Larbi Tebessi - Tébessa (Faculty of Law). It aims to simulate and interpret Algerian legal texts, specifically Administrative Law. The answers generated by the system are for research, educational, and guidance purposes only. They do not constitute formal legal advice and do not replace professional consultations with a qualified attorney registered with the National Union of Algerian Bar Associations (UNOA).
terms.section2Text: While we strive to align the AI model outputs and law index with the legislative texts of the People's Democratic Republic of Algeria, the platform assumes no liability for decisions made based on its generated texts. The only official, legally binding source for Algerian legislation remains the Official Gazette of the Algerian Republic (Journal Officiel de la République Algérienne - JORADP). Users must cross-reference any generated legal article with the official publications.
terms.section3Text: Users must not misuse the platform, attempt unauthorized access, scraping, or load-testing that degrades our infrastructure. Any fraudulent manipulation or cyber intrusion on this platform will be prosecuted under Law No. 09-04 of August 5, 2009, containing rules for prevention and control of offenses linked to information and communication technologies, as well as the relevant provisions of the Algerian Penal Code.
terms.section4Text: These Terms of Service are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the People's Democratic Republic of Algeria. Any dispute arising from the use of this simulator or platform will be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent administrative and judicial courts in Tébessa, Algeria.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How we protect your personal data and interactions with the platform
privacy.intro: At Justivia, we commit to the highest levels of confidentiality and privacy for our users' data, ensuring strict compliance with Law No. 18-07 of June 10, 2018, regarding the protection of physical persons in the processing of personal data in Algeria.
privacy.section1Text: In compliance with Article 4 and Article 24 of Algerian Law No. 18-07, we collect and process only the necessary personal data (such as name, email, and user role) to personalize your experience, enable favorite law bookmarks, and store legal chat history. No excessive data collection is performed.
privacy.section2Text: All legal consultations and prompts sent to the virtual consultant are processed securely using local hosting instances. In accordance with the national sovereignty principles for digital data, your conversations and queries are not transferred outside of the Algerian national territory, and are strictly used to fine-tune the AI model locally without third-party exposure.
privacy.section3Text: Under Section 2 of Law No. 18-07, you hold the right to access (Article 33), rectify (Article 34), and oppose or delete (Article 35 - Right to be forgotten) your personal data. You can exercise these rights directly by deleting your account from your profile settings, which instantly and permanently wipes all your conversations, saved laws, and personal data from our servers.
privacy.section4Text: The processing of personal data on this platform aligns with the regulatory framework monitored by the National Authority for the Protection of Personal Data (Autorité Nationale de Protection des Données à Caractère Personnel - ANPDP) in Algeria. For any inquiries regarding personal data processing, users can consult the ANPDP guidelines.
